query: Build AI agents your whole team can run, and control alternative

results.title: Build Custom AI Agents With Logic & Control
results.content:
No! n8n is built for both technical and non-technical users. With its intuitive drag-and-drop interface, you can create AI agents without writing code. However, developers can dive deeper using custom nodes and scripting when needed.

Yes. n8n prioritizes data privacy and security. You can self-host your instance to retain full control over your data. AI interactions can be configured to limit what data is sent to third-party models, and encrypted connections keep your workflows secure.

AI Agents in n8n don’t just respond to prompts — they take action. While tools like ChatGPT provide text-based outputs, n8n agents can query APIs, update CRMs, send emails, file reports, and more — all autonomously and continuously. [...] Build agents in no time by connecting modular nodes in n8n’s visual builder. Use conditional logic or insert custom code for extra control where it counts. Build a functional prototype in 4 simple steps.

### 1. Create AI Agent Node

 The core of your AI Agent. This node listens for input from a trigger (typically an On Chat Message). Using the Agent’s settings, you define its behaviour via system prompts. Fine-tune parameters using presets or JavaScript. And even add auth to control access.

## Integrations for building AI agents500+ pre-built nodes. Unlimited possibilities. [...] ## FAQ about building AI agents

AI Agents in n8n are autonomous workflows powered by AI that can make decisions, interact with apps, and execute tasks without constant human input. They use a combination of memory, goals, and tools (like web search or database access) to reason through tasks step-by-step. You configure them with a starting prompt and optionally define goals or enable memory so they can build context over time. Once set up, these agents can autonomously execute logic chains and adapt based on responses from AI models like OpenAI or Anthropic.

results.title: The 10 best AI agent builders in 2026 (Tested) | Relay.app
results.content:
If your focus is sales and GTM, Relevance AI was built specifically for that. Its pre-built agent templates for BDR, research, and inbound qualification can get your revenue team up and running fast.

If you're technical and want full control, n8n is the choice for you. Self-host it, customize it, and pay nothing (or very little). The trade-off is complexity. You're on your own for setup and maintenance.

If you want AI agents that truly understand your business, Cassidy is a smart pick. Its Knowledge Base connects your docs, SOPs, and company data so agents respond with real context, not generic AI output. [...] Unified knowledge base: Gain deep business context via a system that continuously syncs across your documents and data sources.
 No-code creation: Enable non-technical teams to build and deploy robust AI agents without developer support.
 Agentic reasoning: Handle nuance, complex logic, and exceptions with AI that makes decisions instead of following rigid scripts.
 Cross-system execution: Execute actions across multiple systems in a single flow by connecting deeply with your software stack.
 Precision and control: Keep full control over business-critical work using guardrails and human-in-the-loop checkpoints.
 Seamless deployment: Deploy agents directly into Slack, Microsoft Teams, Google Chrome, and other tools your team already uses. [...] 1. Relay.app: The best pick for most people. It's the most intuitive way to build reliable AI agents, with best-in-class chat-based editing, access to top AI models with AI credits, and human-in-the-loop controls.
2. Gumloop: A powerful visual canvas for multi-agent orchestration, great for technical teams who want AI-native building.
3. Zapier: Best for teams who need a wide library of integrations (they've got 8,000+ apps!) to connect niche or legacy tools and enterprise-level support.
4. Relevance AI: Purpose-built for sales and GTM teams, with pre-built agent templates for BDR and inbound workflows.
5. n8n: Ideal for developers who want full control and code-level customization on an open-source and self-hostable platform.

## 8 best AI agent builders and tools in 2026

Here are the best AI agent builder:

Alright, let's go over each of these.

### 1. Gumloop

Gumloop

Gumloop is an AI agent builder and automation platform built for enterprise companies. It's used by some big teams at companies like Shopify, Instacart, and Webflow, across multiple organizations like marketing, sales, opations, finance, customer service, HR, and admin.

What sets Gumloop apart from most of the platforms on this list is how teams actually work in it. It's not just a single player tool where one person owns the build locally or on their own account. Anyone on your team with the right permissions can create an agent or skill, and the rest of the org can run it. [...] Here are some things I like about n8n:

Overall, it's a solid choice if you're just getting into building AI agents and you are a technical person. However, if you're looking for a tool that is a bit more user-friendly and can still do everything n8n can, it might be worth looking into an alternative.

results.title: 6 best AI agent frameworks (and how I picked one) in 2026
results.content:
Building AI agent in n8n

I still wanted to include to because if you've used tools like Zapier or Make, and kept hitting their limits, n8n feels like the next step up. It gives you the visual builder experience but with way more flexibility when it comes to custom logic, debugging, and infrastructure control. There's also a built-in AI agent builder with native connectors to different LLM providers and even a LangChain integration, so you can design agents that reason, branch, and take action across your stack.

The big selling point I’ve personally seen for a lot of teams is the self-hosting option. If you're dealing with sensitive data or strict compliance requirements, being able to run everything on your own infrastructure is a major advantage over most other tools in this space. [...] In the broader AI agent framework space, I think of CrewAI as a developer-first alternative to heavier orchestration platforms like LangGraph or AutoGen (which we'll also go over next). It's more opinionated than "just call the API yourself," but still light enough that I feel in control of the code, the tools, and the runtime.

Here are some things I like about CrewAI:

Here are some things that could be improved:

Overall, CrewAI is a promising tool if your main focus is to find an open-source framework for orchestrating agents. However, if you need simpler use cases like AI workflow automation, and you know that not every AI agent needs to be a part of a "team" of other AI agents, then it might be worth looking into an alternative.

#### CrewAI pricing

CrewAI pricing plans [...] If you're an engineer who wants full control over your SDK, memory management, and multi-agent collaboration patterns, LangChain or AutoGen are strong picks. LangChain gives you the most flexibility for custom architectures, especially if you're building RAG applications or need to work across both Python and JavaScript. AutoGen is the move if you care about real-time event-driven orchestration and deep observability into how your agents make decisions.

For teams in regulated industries that need guardrails around how models and data are used, StackAI is one to look into. And if you want something in between, where you get a visual builder but can still drop into code and self-host, n8n and CrewAI both fill that gap well.

results.title: 14 Best AI Agent Builder Platforms 2026 (Ranked, Tested)
results.content:
7-tier RBAC for agent permissions. Deploying agents across a real team requires access control. Taskade ships a 7-tier permission model (Owner, Maintainer, Editor, Commenter, Collaborator, Participant, Viewer). You can let operations run the pipeline agent, engineering edit its prompts, and leadership view its output, all from one permission model.

| Role | Can Edit Agent | Can Run Agent | Can View Output |
 ---  --- |
| Owner | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Maintainer | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Editor | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Commenter | No | Yes | Yes |
| Collaborator | No | Yes | Yes |
| Participant | No | Partial | Yes |
| Viewer | No | No | Yes | [...] ### AI Agents for Project Management in 2026: Build the PM Agent Team (Live Demo)

[...] LangChain plus LangGraph is the best AI agent builder for developers who need full control over state, tools, and orchestration. The Claude Agent SDK is a strong second option for Anthropic-first stacks. OpenAI Assistants API is easiest if you already ship on OpenAI. AutoGen fits Python research teams building multi-agent reasoning loops.

Managed AI agent builders in 2026 typically cost $0 to $99 per user per month. Taskade Agents v2 starts at $6 per month on annual billing, Lindy at around $49, Dust at $29, Stack AI at $199 for teams, and Relevance AI at $199. Open-source tools like LangChain and CrewAI are free but add hosting and engineering time.
